heading: Passenger motor vehicle information
label: 32302 Passenger motor vehicle information

L. 92–513, § 201(c)86 Stat. 95615:1941(d) (1st–13th words)., , ; , , .Oct. 20, 1972July 14, 1976Pub. L. 92–513, § 201(d)86 Stat. 956Pub. L. 94–364, § 20190 Stat. 98132302(b)15:1941(c) (1st–18th and 61st–last words), (d) (14th–last words).32302(c)15:1941(e).In subsection (a), the words before clause (1) are substituted for “The Secretary shall compile the information described in subsection (c) of this section” and “existing information and information to be developed relating to” for clarity and to eliminate unnecessary words.In subsection (b), the words “After the study has been completed” are omitted as executed. The words “To assist a consumer in buying a passenger motor vehicle” are substituted for “so as to be of benefit in their passenger motor vehicle purchasing decisions”, and the words “the Secretary shall provide to the public” are substituted for “the Secretary is authorized and directed to devise specific ways in which . . . can be communicated to consumers” and “furnish it to the public”, to eliminate unnecessary words. The word “existing” is omitted as obsolete.In subsection (c), the words “not later than ” are omitted as executed. The words “prescribe regulations” are substituted for “by rule establish” for consistency in the revised title and because “rule” is synonymous with “regulation”.February 1, 1975

B, title IV, § 24213(a)135 Stat. 826Nov. 15, 2021“Not later than 1 year after the date of enactment of this Act [], the Secretary [of Transportation] shall finalize the proceeding for which comments were requested in the notice entitled ‘New Car Assessment Program’ (80 Fed. Reg. 78522 ()) to update the passenger motor vehicle information required under , United States Code.”Nov. 15, 2021December 16, 2015section 32302(a) of title 49

Improved Vehicle Safety Databases, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–58, div. B, title IV, § 24217135 Stat. 830Nov. 15, 2021“Not later than 3 years after the date of enactment of this Act [], after consultation with frequent users of publicly available databases, the Secretary [of Transportation] shall improve public accessibility to information relating to the publicly accessible vehicle safety databases of the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration by revising the publicly accessible vehicle safety databases—Nov. 15, 2021“(1)to improve organization and functionality, including design features such as drop-down menus;“(2)to allow data from applicable publicly accessible vehicle safety databases to be searched, sorted, aggregated, and downloaded in a manner that—“(A)is consistent with the public interest; and“(B)facilitates easy use by consumers;“(3)to provide greater consistency in presentation of vehicle safety issues;“(4)to improve searchability regarding specific vehicles and issues, which may include the standardization of commonly used search terms; and“(5)to ensure nonconfidential documents and materials relating to information created or obtained by the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration are made publicly available in a manner that is—“(A)timely; and“(B)searchable in databases by any element that the Secretary determines to be in the public interest.”

Consumer Guidance, , , provided that:Pub. L. 114–94, div. B, title XXIV, § 24103(d)129 Stat. 1703Dec. 4, 2015“Not later than 1 year after the date of enactment of this Act [], the Secretary shall make available to the public on the Internet detailed guidance for consumers submitting safety complaints, including—Dec. 4, 2015“(1)a detailed explanation of what information a consumer should include in a complaint; and“(2)a detailed explanation of the possible actions the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration can take to address a complaint and respond to the consumer, including information on—“(A)the consumer records, such as photographs and police reports, that could assist with an investigation; and“(B)the length of time a consumer should retain the records described in subparagraph (A).”

children.text: damage susceptibility.
crashworthiness, crash avoidance, and any other areas the Secretary determines will improve the safety of passenger motor vehicles.
the degree of difficulty of diagnosis and repair of damage to, or failure of, mechanical and electrical systems.

children.heading: .—Motor Vehicle Information
children.label: b .—Motor Vehicle Information
children.text: To assist a consumer in buying a passenger motor vehicle, the Secretary shall provide to the public information developed under subsection (a) of this section. The information shall be in a simple and understandable form that allows comparison of the characteristics referred to in subsection (a)(1)–(3) of this section among the makes and models of passenger motor vehicles. The Secretary may require passenger motor vehicle dealers to distribute the information to prospective buyers. The Secretary, after providing an opportunity for public comment, shall study and report to Congress the most useful data, format, and method for providing simple and understandable damage susceptibility information to consumers.

children.heading: .—Crash Avoidance
children.label: c .—Crash Avoidance
children.text: Not later than 1 year after the date of enactment of the Safety Through Informed Consumers Act of 2015, the Secretary shall promulgate a rule to ensure that crash avoidance information is indicated next to crashworthiness information on stickers placed on motor vehicles by their manufacturers.

children.heading: Motor Vehicle Defect Reporting Information.—
children.label: d Motor Vehicle Defect Reporting Information.—
A
children.text: to affix, in the glove compartment or in another readily accessible location on the vehicle, a sticker, decal, or other device that provides, in simple and understandable language, information about how to submit a safety-related motor vehicle defect complaint to the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration;

children.label: B
children.text: to prominently print the information described in subparagraph (A) within the owner’s manual; and

children.label: C
children.text: to not place such information on the label required under section 3 of the Automobile Information Disclosure Act ().15 U.S.C. 1232

children.heading: .—Application
children.label: 2 .—Application
children.text: The requirements under paragraph (1) shall apply to passenger motor vehicles manufactured in any model year beginning more than 1 year after the date on which a final rule is published under paragraph (1).

children.heading: .—Notice
children.label: 1 .—Notice
children.text: Not later than 1 year after the date of enactment of this subsection, the Secretary shall publish a notice, for purposes of public review and comment, to establish, distinct from crashworthiness information, a means for providing to consumers information relating to advanced crash-avoidance technologies, in accordance with subsection (a).

children.label: i
children.text: determining which advanced crash-avoidance technologies shall be included in the information;

children.label: ii
children.text: developing performance test criteria for use by manufacturers in evaluating advanced crash-avoidance technologies;

children.label: iii
children.text: determining a distinct rating involving each advanced crash-avoidance technology to be included; and

children.label: iv
children.text: updating overall vehicle ratings to incorporate advanced crash-avoidance technology ratings; and

children.label: B
children.text: such other information and analyses as the Secretary determines to be necessary to implement the rating of advanced crash-avoidance technologies.

children.heading: .—Report
children.label: 3 .—Report
children.text: Not later than 18 months after the date of enactment of this subsection, the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives a report that describes a plan for implementing an advanced crash-avoidance technology information and rating system, in accordance with subsection (a).

children.heading: .—Notice
children.label: 1 .—Notice
children.text: Not later than 1 year after the date of enactment of this subsection, the Secretary shall publish a notice, for purposes of public review and comment, to establish a means for providing to consumers information relating to pedestrian, bicyclist, or other vulnerable road user safety technologies, in accordance with subsection (a).

children.label: i
children.text: determining which technologies shall be included in the information;

children.label: ii
children.text: developing performance test criteria for use by manufacturers in evaluating the extent to which automated pedestrian safety systems in light vehicles attempt to prevent and mitigate, to the best extent possible, pedestrian injury;

children.label: iii
children.text: determining a distinct rating involving each technology to be included; and

children.label: iv
children.text: updating overall vehicle ratings to incorporate vulnerable road user safety technology ratings; and

children.label: B
children.text: such other information and analyses as the Secretary determines to be necessary to implement the rating of vulnerable road user safety technologies.

children.heading: .—Report
children.label: 3 .—Report
children.text: Not later than 18 months after the date of enactment of this subsection, the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives a report that describes a plan for implementing an information and rating system for vulnerable road user safety technologies, in accordance with subsection (a).
